page: Vultr voc-s-8c-64gb-1920s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor. It features 8 vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with a single thread per core, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The CPU allocation is shared. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Storage is bundled as a 1920 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance reaching 3138.07, placing it in the top tier (top 10%). Multi-core performance under stress-ng is average (middle 50%) at 25024.21, reflecting its shared CPU allocation. Single-threaded gzip compression achieves 29.39 GB/sec, while single-threaded and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression reach 13.4 GB/sec and 53.07 GB/sec respectively, both aligning with average tier performance.

Given its high memory density of 8.0 GB per core and substantial 1920 GB local NVMe SSD storage, this instance offers a resource-dense profile for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads without the premium of dedicated CPU instances. The shared CPU model represents a tradeoff, making it less suitable for sustained, maximum-load multi-core compute tasks but highly cost-efficient for memory-bound applications. It is well-suited for database hosting, caching layers, memory-intensive data processing, and general-purpose workloads requiring large local storage.
description: Vultr voc-s-8c-64gb-1920s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring an 8-core AMD EPYC Milan processor (x86_64 architecture, shared CPU allocation) running at 2.0 GHz. It is configured with 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high 8.0 GB per core ratio, and includes a 1920 GB local NVMe SSD. While its multi-core stress-ng benchmark of 25024.21 and bzip3 decompression speeds fall into the average tier, its single-core stress-ng performance of 3138.07 ranks in the top tier (top 10%). This resource profile makes the instance a cost-effective option for memory-intensive and storage-heavy workloads. It is highly suitable for database hosting, caching layers, and memory-bound data processing tasks that benefit from large local NVMe storage but do not require dedicated CPU resources.
og_description: Vultr voc-s-8c-64gb-1920s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance with an 8-core AMD EPYC Milan CPU, 64 GB RAM, and a 1920 GB NVMe SSD, offering top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-s-8c-64gb-1920s-amd features 8 AMD EPYC Milan cores, 64 GB RAM, and a 1920 GB NVMe SSD for memory-intensive and database workloads.
tagline: An AMD-powered instance combining high memory density and a spacious NVMe SSD for memory-bound and storage-intensive workloads.

64.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio
1920 GB of local NVMe SSD storage bundled
